description: Remove the unused `EdFi.DataManagementService.Core` project reference from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.csproj`. Acceptance: `Backend.RelationalModel` no longer references `Core` unless a source file in that project has a concrete compile-time dependency on a `Core` type, the project still builds cleanly, and downstream projects that consume `Backend.RelationalModel` continue to compile without relying on a transitive `Core` reference from this project.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the unnecessary `<ProjectReference Include="..\..\core\EdFi.DataManagementService.Core\EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.csproj" />` entry is removed from `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.RelationalModel.csproj` unless a new code dependency was intentionally introduced.

description: Synchronize the DMS Docker environment templates for the new startup validation and startup status settings. Add `DMS_VALIDATE_PROVISIONED_MAPPINGS_ON_STARTUP=false` to `eng/docker-compose/.env.e2e` and add `DMS_STARTUP_STATUS_FILE_PATH=/tmp/dms-startup-status.json` to `eng/docker-compose/.env.example`. Acceptance: both `.env` files explicitly define both variables, and every variable referenced by `eng/docker-compose/local-dms.yml` and `eng/docker-compose/published-dms.yml` has a matching declaration in both environment templates.
steps-to-verify: Confirm `eng/docker-compose/.env.e2e` and `eng/docker-compose/.env.example` both contain `DMS_VALIDATE_PROVISIONED_MAPPINGS_ON_STARTUP` and `DMS_STARTUP_STATUS_FILE_PATH` with the intended default values.

description: Update `reference/design/backend-redesign/epics/15-plan-compilation/startup-failure-status-surfacing.md` so the documented startup status contract matches the phases currently written by the application. Acceptance: the document either explicitly lists all emitted phase names (`ConfigureServices`, `BuildApplication`, `LoadDmsInstances`, `InitializeDatabase`, `InitializeApiSchemas`, `InitializeBackendMappings`, `WarmUpOidcMetadataCache`, `ConfigureEndpoints`, and `Ready`) or clearly states that the phase list is illustrative and includes the currently implemented additional phases.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the design document reflects the phase names defined in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Infrastructure/StartupPhaseExecutor.cs` and used in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Program.cs`.

description: Resolve the assembly ownership mismatch for `EffectiveSchemaSetBuilder`. Acceptance: Move it into the `Core`-owned namespace and update all consumers. Remove any compensating project references that were only added to support the mismatch.
steps-to-verify: Confirm `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Startup/EffectiveSchemaSetBuilder.cs` uses a namespace and assembly placement that make ownership obvious, and that all consuming files import the updated namespace.

description: Add unit coverage for `PostgresqlRuntimeMappingSetCompiler.CompileAsync` when the authoritative effective schema key changes between key derivation and compilation. Acceptance: a unit test in an existing test project with access to `Old.Postgresql` internals configures `IEffectiveSchemaSetProvider` to return different effective schema data across calls, invokes the mismatch path, and asserts that `CompileAsync` throws `InvalidOperationException` with a message that identifies both the expected and actual mapping set keys.
steps-to-verify: Add a test named in the existing NUnit style for the mismatch scenario and verify it fails before the implementation is correct and passes afterward.

description: Document the intentional rethrow after fatal startup exit handling in `StartupPhaseExecutor.RunFatalAsync`. Acceptance: the catch block or `HandleFatalFailure` call path includes a concise comment explaining that the rethrow exists for testability because production `IStartupProcessExit` terminates the process before control returns, and existing unit tests still validate the behavior.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the comment appears adjacent to the `throw;` statement in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Infrastructure/StartupPhaseExecutor.cs` and accurately describes the production-versus-test control flow.

description: Improve diagnostics for bootstrap-stage startup status file write failures before DI logging is available. Acceptance: the pre-host `FileStartupStatusSignal` path used by `Program.cs` no longer swallows write failures silently when the status file cannot be created or updated. Bootstrap failures should emit a visible diagnostic through a bootstrap logger or a targeted `Console.Error` fallback while preserving the existing runtime logger-based behavior after DI is built.
steps-to-verify: Confirm the bootstrap code path in `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Program.cs` and `src/dms/frontend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Frontend.AspNetCore/Infrastructure/StartupStatusSignal.cs` emits a visible warning when the configured startup status file path is invalid or unwritable before the app host is built.

description: Make `LoadAndBuildEffectiveSchemaTask` leave a cleaner failure state if provider initialization fails partway through. Acceptance: initialization no longer leaves `IEffectiveApiSchemaProvider` initialized while `IEffectiveSchemaSetProvider` remains uninitialized when the second provider initialization throws. The implementation must either initialize the schema-set provider before the effective API schema provider or explicitly roll back the first initialization on failure. Add unit coverage for the partial-failure scenario and verify the task surfaces the original exception.
steps-to-verify: Confirm `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core/Startup/LoadAndBuildEffectiveSchemaTask.cs` no longer initializes the effective API schema provider first without compensating cleanup when `IEffectiveSchemaSetProvider.Initialize(...)` fails.

Add or update unit tests in `src/dms/core/EdFi.DataManagementService.Core.Tests.Unit/Startup/LoadAndBuildEffectiveSchemaTaskTests.cs` to simulate a failure during schema-set provider initialization and assert the final provider state is not partially initialized.

description: Clarify the failure message for unsupported transitive `UnifiedAlias` resolution in `ReferenceIdentityProjectionLogicalFieldResolver`. Acceptance: when a grouped reference identity binding resolves to a canonical column whose storage is itself `ColumnStorage.UnifiedAlias`, the thrown exception explicitly states that transitive `UnifiedAlias` resolution is not supported, identifies both alias columns involved, and preserves the existing context about the reference object path, table, and logical field.
steps-to-verify: Confirm `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans/ReferenceIdentityProjectionLogicalFieldResolver.cs` emits a targeted error message for alias-of-alias resolution instead of the generic "column is not stored" message.

Add or update a unit test in `src/dms/backend/EdFi.DataManagementService.Backend.Plans.Tests.Unit/` that constructs a transitive alias scenario and asserts the thrown message mentions unsupported transitive `UnifiedAlias` resolution and both column names.
